info.title: Stepflow API
info.description: API for Stepflow flows and runs

get.description: Returns the current health status of the service. Use the `error` query parameter to trigger test error responses.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Trigger an error for testing error response format

schema.description: Trigger an error for testing error response format

post.description: Store a blob and return its content-based ID. Supports two content types: `application/json` (JSON body with `data`, `blobType`, and optional `filename` fields) and `application/octet-stream` (raw binary body, use `X-Blob-Filename` header for filename).

get.description: Retrieve a blob by its content-based ID. Supports content negotiation via `Accept` header: `application/json` (default) returns JSON with data, blobType, blobId, and filename; `application/octet-stream` returns raw bytes.
List all available components from registered plugins.

parameters.name: includeSchemas
parameters.description: Whether to include schemas in the response (default: true)

schema.description: Whether to include schemas in the response (default: true)

get.description: List executions with optional status, flow name, and hierarchy filtering.

parameters.name: rootRunId
parameters.description: Filter to runs under this root (includes the root itself)

schema.description: Filter to runs under this root (includes the root itself)

parameters.name: parentRunId
parameters.description: Filter to direct children of this parent run

schema.description: Filter to direct children of this parent run

parameters.name: rootsOnly
parameters.description: Filter to only root runs (runs where parent_run_id is None)

schema.description: Filter to only root runs (runs where parent_run_id is None)

parameters.name: maxDepth
parameters.description: Maximum depth for hierarchy queries (0 = root only)

schema.description: Maximum depth for hierarchy queries (0 = root only)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

schema.description: Maximum number of results to return

post.summary: Create and execute a flow run
post.description: Create and execute a flow by hash. Supports both single and batch execution. By default, returns immediately with 202 Accepted. Set `wait: true` to block until the run completes and return 200 OK with the result.

requestBody.description:
Request to create/execute a flow.

The `input` field is always an array of input values:
- Single-item array `[value]`: Executes one run with `value` as input
- Multi-item array `[v1, v2, ...]`: Executes multiple runs (batch mode)

This design avoids ambiguity: to run a workflow with an array as input,
wrap it in another array: `[[1, 2, 3]]` runs once with input `[1, 2, 3]`.

get.description: Returns the current run status and details. Use `wait=true` to long-poll until the run reaches a terminal state (completed, failed, or cancelled).

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: If true, wait for the run to reach a terminal state before responding.

schema.description: If true, wait for the run to reach a terminal state before responding.

parameters.name: timeoutSecs
parameters.description:
Maximum seconds to wait when wait=true (default 300). If the timeout elapses,
returns the current run status rather than an error.

schema.description:
Maximum seconds to wait when wait=true (default 300). If the timeout elapses,
returns the current run status rather than an error.

200.description:
Detailed flow run information including item details.

For completed runs, `item_details` contains per-item information including
inputs and step statuses. For active runs, `item_details` may be `None`
(query the owning orchestrator for live status).

delete.description: Delete a completed execution by ID. Running executions cannot be deleted.

409.description: Run is still running and cannot be deleted

get.summary: Get all item results for a run
get.description: Returns results for all items in the run, ordered by item index. For single-item runs (item_count=1), returns a single item.

get.summary: Get the workflow definition for a run
get.description: Retrieve the workflow definition associated with a specific run.

200.description: Response containing a flow definition and its hash for run endpoints

get.description: Get step-level execution details for a specific run. Use `item_index` to get statuses for a specific item in batch runs. Without `item_index`, statuses are aggregated across all items.

parameters.name: itemIndex
parameters.description:
Optional item index for multi-item (batch) runs.
If not specified, step statuses are aggregated across all items
using "worst status" precedence (Failed > Running > Runnable > Blocked > Skipped > Completed).

schema.description:
Optional item index for multi-item (batch) runs.
If not specified, step statuses are aggregated across all items
using "worst status" precedence (Failed > Running > Runnable > Blocked > Skipped > Completed).

get.summary: Get detailed status for a specific step
get.description: Returns the detailed status, component, and result for a specific step. Use `asof` with a journal sequence number from the SSE stream to ensure read-after-write consistency. Returns 409 if the metadata store has not yet caught up to the requested sequence number.

parameters.name: asof
parameters.description:
Minimum journal sequence number. If the step status in metadata has
a `journal_seqno` less than this value, the server returns 409 Conflict
indicating the read is not yet consistent with the SSE stream position.

schema.description:
Minimum journal sequence number. If the step status in metadata has
a `journal_seqno` less than this value, the server returns 409 Conflict
indicating the read is not yet consistent with the SSE stream position.

200.description:
Detailed step status entry with result and journal tracking.

Stored in the `step_statuses` metadata table. Each entry tracks a single
step's status for a specific item, along with the journal sequence number
that produced the change (enabling `?asof=N` consistent reads).

409.description: Metadata not yet consistent with requested sequence number

get.summary: Stream run execution events via SSE
get.description: Opens a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream of execution events for a run. Events are streamed in journal order with the SSE `id` set to the journal sequence number. Use `since` to resume from a specific point. The stream closes after a `run_completed` event for the requested run.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description:
Journal sequence number to start from (inclusive). If omitted,
starts from the beginning of the run.

schema.description:
Journal sequence number to start from (inclusive). If omitted,
starts from the beginning of the run.

parameters.name: includeSubRuns
parameters.description: Include events from sub-runs (default: false, only root run events).

schema.description: Include events from sub-runs (default: false, only root run events).

parameters.name: eventTypes
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of event types to include (e.g., "step_started,step_completed").
If omitted, all event types are included.

schema.description:
Comma-separated list of event types to include (e.g., "step_started,step_completed").
If omitted, all event types are included.

parameters.name: includeResults
parameters.description: Include result payloads in step_completed and item_completed events (default: false).

schema.description: Include result payloads in step_completed and item_completed events (default: false).

200.description: Server-Sent Events stream of execution events

post.description: Cancel a running execution by ID.

200.description: Summary information about a flow run.

post.description: Store a flow and return its content-based hash ID.

200.description: Response when a flow is stored

get.description: Retrieve a flow definition by its content-based hash ID.

200.description: Response containing a flow definition and its ID

delete.description: Delete a flow by its content-based hash ID.

get.summary: Get variable schema for a flow
get.description: Retrieve the variable schema for a flow, including environment variable mappings. This is a lightweight alternative to fetching the entire flow when you only need variable information.

200.description: Response containing variable schema and environment variable mappings for a flow.

parentRunId.description: The parent run that spawned this sub-run.

flowId.description: The flow being executed by the sub-run.

itemCount.description: Number of items in the sub-run.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.
Timestamp when health was checked (RFC3339 format)

ErrorResponse.description:
Error response structure.

Server handlers should return this, but usually it is better to create it
by returning an `error_stack::Report<ServerError>` and using the automatic
conversion to `ErrorResponse`.

Other `error_stack::Report` types will automatically convert to internal errors.

ErrorStackEntry.description: A single entry in an error stack for detailed error reporting

attachments.description: Additional context attached to this error

StoreBlobResponse.description: Response when a blob is stored

filename.description: The filename if one was provided

BlobId.description: A SHA-256 hash of the blob content, represented as a hexadecimal string.

StoreBlobRequest.description: Request to store a blob (JSON mode)

blobType.description: The type of blob (data or flow). Defaults to "data".

filename.description: Optional filename to associate with the blob.

BlobType.description:
Type of blob stored in the blob store.

Cases:
* `flow`: A workflow/flow definition
* `data`: Generic data blob
* `binary`: Raw binary data (base64-encoded in JSON transport)

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

GetBlobResponse.description: Response when retrieving a blob (JSON mode)

filename.description: The filename if one was set

result.description: The item result. Only included when `include_results=true`.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

input_schema.description:
The input schema for the component.

Can be any valid JSON schema (object, primitive, array, etc.).

output_schema.description:
The output schema for the component.

Can be any valid JSON schema (object, primitive, array, etc.).

Component.description: Identifies a specific plugin and atomic functionality to execute. Use component name for builtins (e.g., 'eval') or path format for plugins (e.g., '/python/udf').

StatusEventStepReady.title: StatusEventStepReady
StatusEventStepReady.description: A step's dependencies are satisfied and it is ready to execute.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

ExecutionStatus.description:
Status of a workflow execution

Cases:
* `running`: Execution is currently running
* `completed`: Execution completed successfully
* `failed`: Execution failed with an error
* `cancelled`: Execution was cancelled by user request
* `paused`: Execution is paused (debug mode)
* `recoveryFailed`: Execution failed during recovery after orchestrator restart

RunSummary.description: Summary information about a flow run.

items.description: Statistics about items in this run.

rootRunId.description:
Root run ID for this execution tree.

For top-level runs, this equals `run_id`.
For sub-flows, this is the original run that started the tree.

parentRunId.description:
Parent run ID if this is a sub-flow.

None for top-level runs, Some(parent_id) for sub-flows.

orchestratorId.description:
The orchestrator currently managing this run.

None means the run is orphaned (no orchestrator owns it).
Set when the run is created and updated during recovery.

createdAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the event that created this run.

Records where in the journal this run was created, enabling efficient
metadata queries during recovery (filter by offset range).

finishedAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the RunCompleted event for this run.

Set when the run reaches a terminal state. `None` means the run is
still in progress (or completed before this field was added).

ItemStatistics.description: Statistics about items in a run.

total.description: Total number of items in this run.

CreateRunRequest.description:
Request to create/execute a flow.

The `input` field is always an array of input values:
- Single-item array `[value]`: Executes one run with `value` as input
- Multi-item array `[v1, v2, ...]`: Executes multiple runs (batch mode)

This design avoids ambiguity: to run a workflow with an array as input,
wrap it in another array: `[[1, 2, 3]]` runs once with input `[1, 2, 3]`.

input.description: Input data for the flow - always an array (one element per run)

overrides.description: Optional workflow overrides to apply before execution

variables.description: Optional variables to provide for variable references in the workflow

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum concurrency for batch execution (only used when input is an array)

wait.description:
If true, block until the run completes and return the result (200 OK).
If false (default), return immediately with status Running (202 Accepted).

timeoutSecs.description:
Maximum seconds to wait when wait=true (default 300). If the timeout elapses,
returns the current run status rather than an error.

Value.description: Any JSON value (object, array, string, number, boolean, or null)

WorkflowOverrides.description:
Workflow overrides that can be applied to modify step behavior at runtime.

Overrides are keyed by step ID and contain merge patches or other transformation
specifications to modify step properties before execution.

steps.description: Map of step ID to override specification

StepOverride.description:
Override specification for a single step.

Contains the override type (merge patch, json patch, etc.) and the value
to apply. The type field uses `$type` to avoid collisions with step properties.

$type.description: The type of override to apply. Defaults to "merge_patch" if not specified.

value.description: The override value to apply, interpreted based on the override type.

OverrideType.description:
The type of override operation to perform.

Cases:
* `merge_patch`: Apply a JSON Merge Patch (RFC 7396) to the step.

This is the default override type. The value should be a JSON object
where null values indicate fields to remove and other values are merged
into the target step.
* `json_patch`: Apply a JSON Patch (RFC 6902) to the step. (Future extension)

The value should be an array of JSON Patch operations.
This is reserved for future use.

CreateRunResponse.description:
Response for create run operations.

Shares the same base fields as `RunDetails` (GET /runs/{id}) via `RunSummary`,
with optional item results when `wait=true`.

items.description: Statistics about items in this run.

rootRunId.description:
Root run ID for this execution tree.

For top-level runs, this equals `run_id`.
For sub-flows, this is the original run that started the tree.

parentRunId.description:
Parent run ID if this is a sub-flow.

None for top-level runs, Some(parent_id) for sub-flows.

orchestratorId.description:
The orchestrator currently managing this run.

None means the run is orphaned (no orchestrator owns it).
Set when the run is created and updated during recovery.

createdAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the event that created this run.

Records where in the journal this run was created, enabling efficient
metadata queries during recovery (filter by offset range).

finishedAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the RunCompleted event for this run.

Set when the run reaches a terminal state. `None` means the run is
still in progress (or completed before this field was added).

results.description:
Item results, only populated when `wait=true` and the run has completed.
Each entry contains the item's index, status, and result.

ItemResult.description: Result for an individual item in a multi-item run.

itemIndex.description: Index of this item in the input array (0-based).

result.description: Result of this item, if completed.

FlowResultFailed.description: The step failed with the given error.

FlowError.description: An error reported from within a flow or step.

StatusEventStepCompleted.title: StatusEventStepCompleted
StatusEventStepCompleted.description: A step has completed (successfully or with failure).

status.description: Whether the step succeeded or failed.

result.description: The step result. Only included when `include_results=true`.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

RunDetails.description:
Detailed flow run information including item details.

For completed runs, `item_details` contains per-item information including
inputs and step statuses. For active runs, `item_details` may be `None`
(query the owning orchestrator for live status).

items.description: Statistics about items in this run.

rootRunId.description:
Root run ID for this execution tree.

For top-level runs, this equals `run_id`.
For sub-flows, this is the original run that started the tree.

parentRunId.description:
Parent run ID if this is a sub-flow.

None for top-level runs, Some(parent_id) for sub-flows.

orchestratorId.description:
The orchestrator currently managing this run.

None means the run is orphaned (no orchestrator owns it).
Set when the run is created and updated during recovery.

createdAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the event that created this run.

Records where in the journal this run was created, enabling efficient
metadata queries during recovery (filter by offset range).

finishedAtSeqno.description:
Journal sequence number of the RunCompleted event for this run.

Set when the run reaches a terminal state. `None` means the run is
still in progress (or completed before this field was added).

itemDetails.description:
Item details with inputs and step statuses.
- `None`: details not requested, or run is active (query executor)
- `Some`: item-level details available

overrides.description: Optional workflow overrides applied to this run (per-run, not per-item).

ItemDetails.description:
Detailed information for a single item in a run.

Includes the item's input, execution status, and step-level details.

itemIndex.description: Index of this item in the input array (0-based).

StepStatusInfo.description: Status information for a single step execution.

StepStatus.description:
Status of an individual step within a workflow

Cases:
* `blocked`: Step is waiting for dependencies to complete
* `runnable`: Step is ready to be executed
* `running`: Step is currently executing
* `completed`: Step has been executed successfully
* `skipped`: Step was skipped due to conditions
* `failed`: Step failed with an error

itemCount.description: Total number of items in this run

items.description: Individual item results ordered by item index

RunFlowResponse.description: Response containing a flow definition and its hash for run endpoints

Flow.description:
A workflow consisting of a sequence of steps and their outputs.

A flow represents a complete workflow that can be executed. It contains:
- A sequence of steps to execute
- Named outputs that can reference step outputs

Flows should not be cloned. They should generally be stored and passed as a
reference or inside an `Arc`.

schemas.description:
Consolidated schema information for the flow.
Contains input/output schemas, step output schemas, and shared `$defs`.

steps.description: The steps to execute for the flow.

output.description: The outputs of the flow, mapping output names to their values.

examples.description: Example inputs for the workflow that can be used for testing and UI dropdowns.

metadata.description: Extensible metadata for the flow that can be used by tools and frameworks.

FlowSchema.description: A JSON Schema object describing the flow's type information. Contains input/output schemas, variable schemas, and step output schemas wrapped in standard JSON Schema format with type, properties, and $defs.

Step.description:
A step in a workflow that executes a component with specific arguments.

Note: Step output schemas are stored in the flow's `types.steps` field,
not on individual steps. This allows for shared `$defs` and avoids duplication.

component.description: The component to execute in this step

input.description: Arguments to pass to the component for this step

mustExecute.description:
If true, this step must execute even if its output is not used by the workflow output.
Useful for steps with side effects (e.g., writing to databases, sending notifications).

metadata.description: Extensible metadata for the step that can be used by tools and frameworks.

ErrorAction.description: Error action determines what happens when a step fails.

ValueExpr.description: A value expression: any JSON value (null, boolean, number, string, array, or object). Objects with reserved $-prefixed keys are interpreted as expression references: {"$step": "id", "path"?: "..."}, {"$input": "path"}, {"$variable": "path", "default"?: ValueExpr}, {"$literal": value}, {"$if": cond, "then": expr, "else"?: expr}, {"$coalesce": [expr, ...]}. See https://stepflow.org/docs/flows/expressions for details.

configFile.description:
Path to an external stepflow config file for tests.
Relative paths are resolved from the workflow file's directory.
Mutually exclusive with `config` - validated at runtime.

config.description:
Inline stepflow configuration for tests.
Mutually exclusive with `config_file` - validated at runtime.

TestCase.description: A single test case for a workflow.

name.description: Unique identifier for the test case.

description.description: Optional description of what this test case verifies.

input.description: Input data for the workflow in this test case.

output.description: Expected output from the workflow for this test case.

ExampleInput.description: An example input for a workflow that can be used in UI dropdowns.

name.description: Name of the example input for display purposes.

description.description: Optional description of what this example demonstrates.

input.description: The input data for this example.

StatusEventRunInitialized.title: StatusEventRunInitialized
StatusEventRunInitialized.description: A run was initialized and its steps have been discovered.

steps.description: Step names needed per item (outer index = item_index).

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

steps.description: Dictionary of step run results keyed by step ID

stepId.description: Step ID (the stable identifier for this step in the workflow)

component.description: Component name/URL that this step executes

result.description: The result of the step execution (if completed)

StatusEventRunCreated.title: StatusEventRunCreated
StatusEventRunCreated.description: A run was created with its initial configuration.

sequenceNumber.description: Journal sequence number for this event.

timestamp.description: Timestamp when this event was recorded.

OnErrorRetry.title: OnErrorRetry
OnErrorRetry.description:
If the step fails, retry it.

`max_retries` limits retries due to component errors — cases where the
component ran and returned an error. Transport-level failures (subprocess
crashes, network errors) are retried separately according to the plugin's
retry configuration and do not count against this budget.

maxRetries.description:
Maximum number of retries due to component errors (default: 3).

Total attempts for component errors = max_retries + 1 (initial).

StepStatusEntry.description:
Detailed step status entry with result and journal tracking.

Stored in the `step_statuses` metadata table. Each entry tracks a single
step's status for a specific item, along with the journal sequence number
that produced the change (enabling `?asof=N` consistent reads).

stepIndex.description: Step index in the workflow definition.

result.description: Step result (only present when completed or failed).

journalSeqno.description: Journal sequence number of the event that produced this status.

updatedAt.description: When this status was last updated.

OnErrorDefault.title: OnErrorDefault
OnErrorDefault.description:
If the step fails, use the `defaultValue` instead.
If `defaultValue` is not specified, the step returns null.
The default value must be a literal JSON value (not an expression).
For dynamic defaults, use `$coalesce` in the consuming expression instead.

StatusEvent.description:
A status event with metadata from the execution journal.

Wraps a [`StatusEventKind`] with the journal sequence number and timestamp,
providing a flat JSON structure via `#[serde(flatten)]`.

dryRun.description: If true, only validate the flow without storing it

StoreFlowResponse.description: Response when a flow is stored

flowId.description: The ID of the flow (computed from content hash, always present)

stored.description: Whether the flow was actually stored (false for dry_run or if validation has fatal errors)

Diagnostics.description: Collection of diagnostics with utility methods

Diagnostic.description:
A diagnostic with error code, message, path, and metadata.

Created via the `diagnostic!` macro with optional builder methods:

```ignore
diagnostic!(DiagnosticKind::DuplicateStepId, "Duplicate step ID '{step_id}'", { step_id })
    .at(path)
    .experimental()
```

## JSON Format

```json
{
  "kind": "duplicateStepId",
  "code": 3000,
  "level": "fatal",
  "formatted": "Duplicate step ID 'foo'",
  "data": { "stepId": "foo" },
  "path": "$.steps[0]",
  "experimental": false
}
```

data.description: Structured data with camelCase keys (null if no data)

path.description: JSON path to the field with the issue

experimental.description: Whether this diagnostic is experimental (may have false positives)

DiagnosticLevel.description:
Diagnostic level indicating severity and impact

Cases:
* `fatal`: Fatal: Prevents analysis from proceeding
* `error`: Error: Will definitely fail during execution
* `warning`: Warning: Likely to cause problems during execution

Path.description: Path to a location in the workflow definition, serialized as a string

OnErrorFail.title: OnErrorFail
OnErrorFail.description: If the step fails, the flow will fail.

FlowResponse.description: Response containing a flow definition and its ID

allExamples.description: All available examples (includes both examples and test cases)

FlowVariablesResponse.description: Response containing variable schema and environment variable mappings for a flow.

schema.description: The raw variable JSON Schema (null if the flow has no variables)

envVars.description:
Mapping from variable name to the environment variable that populates it.
Only includes variables that have an `env_var` annotation.
